note: Carded after a failed attempt on 2026-07-20 was fully reverted. Native Electron drag-out (startDrag) worked - real filenames, multi-file drop - but the e.preventDefault() it required killed the shared HTML5 dragstart that in-app drops ride on, so dragging cards inside the library stopped reaching the prompt and landed as imported cards. Reverted all three files to HEAD; tree clean, nothing shipped. Root process failure: skipped the Context Router (components.md / gallery.md / events.md) and never asked what else consumed that dragstart. Card instructs the next agent to read the docs FIRST. Open and unexplained: pressing Cancel on the Windows file-conflict dialog exited the app, with no trace in app.log because main.js has no render-process-gone or uncaughtException handler.

SINGLE-card drag-out implemented via Chromium DownloadURL. Root of prior revert confirmed by routing the docs: preventDefault on the shared card dragstart nukes the HTML5 drag that in-app drag-to-prompt rides. Fix avoids preventDefault/startDrag/IPC entirely - set DownloadURL (mime:realname:origin/project-file?path=abs) ALONGSIDE application/mpi-media on the same drag in both dragstart handlers (image + video). Real filename from extractFilenameFromPath. 3 edits in MpiGalleryGrid.js (~15 lines), no other files. Multi-select export dropped per user. Awaiting live drag-to-Explorer test.

User-verified live: single card dragged to an OS folder lands with its real Media-folder filename; drag-to-prompt intact. Shipped via Chromium DownloadURL alongside application/mpi-media on the same HTML5 drag - no preventDefault/startDrag/IPC. One file touched (MpiGalleryGrid.js). Multi-select export dropped by user. validation.md = PASS.
